title: ALO — Stack Health
description: Operational health of the ALO pipeline: gateway, analyzer, and logstash.

panels.title: Pipeline Flow Rates
panels.description: Events/sec at each pipeline stage. If input >> output, the pipeline is bottlenecked.

panels.title: Queue Depth
panels.description: Events waiting in Logstash queue. Growing = input faster than processing capacity.

panels.title: Plugin Time per Event
panels.description: Avg ms per event by plugin type. Shows whether the HTTP filter (analyzer) or ES output is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Analyzer In-Progress & Latency
panels.description: Concurrent requests to analyzer + avg response time. High in-progress = analyzer is saturated.

panels.title: Worker Util
panels.description: Logstash pipeline worker utilization %

panels.title: Backpressure
panels.description: Queue backpressure (0 = healthy, >0 = input blocked)

panels.title: Analyzer Load
panels.description: In-progress requests to analyzer

panels.title: Gateways UP
panels.description: Number of healthy gateway instances over time. A drop means instances went down.

panels.title: Connection States
panels.description: Aggregated across selected instances. High 'reading' may indicate slow clients; high 'writing' may indicate slow upstream.

panels.title: Dropped Connections
panels.description: Sum of (accepted - handled) across all instances. Should always be zero. Non-zero means worker_connections limit was hit on one or more instances.

panels.title: Error Rate
panels.description: Percentage of non-2xx responses on /analyze. The analyzer is designed to always return 200 — any errors here indicate a real issue.

panels.title: Latency (P50 / P95 / P99)
panels.description: Request duration percentiles aggregated across selected instances.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Analyzer process CPU usage per instance.

panels.title: Memory Usage
panels.description: Analyzer process resident memory

panels.title: Events In / Out Rate
panels.description: Aggregated throughput. Output should track input; a growing gap means events are backing up.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Logstash process CPU utilization

panels.title: Queue Backpressure
panels.description: Pipeline queue backpressure flow metric

panels.title: Output Errors
panels.description: Elasticsearch bulk request errors and non-retryable document failures. Any sustained growth means data loss.

panels.title: JVM Heap Usage
panels.description: Logstash JVM heap used vs max over time

panels.title: JVM Non-Heap Memory
panels.description: Logstash non-heap memory (class metadata, code cache)

panels.title: Process Memory (Virtual)
panels.description: Logstash total virtual memory

panels.title: GC Pressure
panels.description: Old-gen GC time rate — the #1 cause of Logstash freezes. If old GC runs >5% of wall time, the pipeline is in trouble.

panels.title: Avg Worker Utilization
panels.description: Average worker utilization across selected instances. Near 100% = saturated.

panels.title: Max JVM Heap Used
panels.description: Worst-case JVM heap across selected instances. Alert at >85%.

panels.title: Open FDs
panels.description: Worst-case file descriptor usage across instances. Approaching max = Logstash will crash.

panels.title: Pipelines UP
panels.description: Number of healthy pipeline instances over time.

panels.title: JVM Threads
panels.description: Logstash JVM thread count

panels.title: DLQ Document Count
panels.description: Documents in the dead-letter index. Growth means the analyzer is producing unindexable records.

panels.title: DLQ Documents (Recent)
panels.description: Recent dead-letter documents. Shows what was rejected and why.

panels.title: ES CPU
panels.description: Elasticsearch process CPU usage

panels.title: ES JVM Heap
panels.description: Elasticsearch JVM heap used vs max

panels.title: ES GC Rate
panels.description: Elasticsearch garbage collection rate

panels.title: ES JVM Memory Pools
panels.description: Per-pool memory usage

panels.title: ES Thread Pool Queues
panels.description: Queued operations in Elasticsearch. Growing queues = ES is the bottleneck.

panels.title: ES Rejected Operations
panels.description: Rejected thread pool tasks per second. Non-zero = ES is overloaded and dropping work.

panels.title: ES Write Queue
panels.description: Elasticsearch write thread pool queue

links.title: Stress Analysis
links.tooltip: Jump to ALO Stress Analysis dashboard
